segments.title: Fix `useId()` mismatch between SSR and client-side rendering
segments.summary: The PR addresses a `React.useId` hydration mismatch between server-side and client-side rendering. The previous single-pass rendering architecture caused non-deterministic ID generation. The fix implements a new two-phase rendering model that uses isolated renders for the application and document shell, stitching the resulting streams together. This guarantees deterministic `useId` generation and makes the `<Document>` component a true React Server Component.
decisions: Re-architect the server-side rendering pipeline to use a two-phase 'Nested Renders with Stream Stitching' model.
segments.retrieval_summary: Fix React.useId hydration mismatch via two-phase rendering with stream stitching by justinvdm

segments.title: Potential for `useId` collision in concurrent renders
segments.summary: A reviewer pointed out a potential issue where the new concurrent server rendering process could generate duplicate `useId`s. The author acknowledged the problem and planned to fix it by adding different ID prefixes to each render stream.
actions: justinvdm: add different id prefixes to solve potential `useId` collision.
decisions: Add unique ID prefixes to concurrent render streams to prevent `useId` collisions.
segments.retrieval_summary: Plan add unique ID prefixes to prevent useId collision in concurrent renders per Tobbe review
